purpose: Human-eval candidate batch — NOT filed to the library. Span many dimensions/archetypes on one topic so feedback targets question *form*. Rate freely; verdicts feed SKILL.md rules + an exemplar bank.

candidates.q: Which country with no coastline at all is the largest by land?
candidates.note: ~2.7M km²; finalists Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Chad, Niger.

candidates.q: Counting all its overseas lands, which country touches the most time zones?
candidates.note: Beats Russia & USA (11 each) thanks to territories like French Polynesia, Réunion, French Guiana.

candidates.q: Which country that shares no land border with any neighbor has the most people?
candidates.note: Filter knocks out mainland giants; finalists Japan, Philippines, UK, Madagascar.

candidates.q: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
candidates.note: ~202,000 km, far ahead; finalists Canada, Indonesia, Russia, Norway.

candidates.q: In which country is the highest natural ground still barely above the sea — the lowest 'high point' of any nation?
candidates.note: World's flattest country, averaging ~1.5 m; finalists Maldives, Tuvalu, Kiribati.

candidates.q: Which country became the world's newest widely recognized nation, breaking away in 2011?
candidates.note: Finalists South Sudan, Montenegro (2006), Kosovo (2008), East Timor (2002).

candidates.q: Which country's main seat of government sits higher up a mountainside than any other capital?
candidates.note: Contested: La Paz is the de-facto capital; Sucre is constitutional. Finalists La Paz, Quito, Bogotá, Thimphu.

candidates.q: Of all the countries with a seat at the United Nations, which covers the least land?
candidates.note: Filter excludes Vatican City (not a UN member). Finalists Monaco, Nauru, Tuvalu, San Marino.

candidates.q: Which country has more ancient pyramids than any other?
candidates.note: More than double Egypt's ~138, built by the Kingdom of Kush. Finalists Sudan, Egypt, Mexico.

candidates.q: Which country has the northernmost capital city of any independent nation?
Among countries big enough to make the main economic rankings, which produces the most wealth per person?
candidates.note: Tiny Monaco/Liechtenstein rank higher but are often excluded from IMF lists. Finalists Luxembourg, Ireland, Switzerland, Norway.

candidates.q: Which country's largest city sits partly in Europe and partly in Asia, split by a narrow strait?
Which country has the most islands — over a quarter of a million of them?
candidates.note: Glaciation-carved skerries; Norway ~239,000 is close. Contested by counting method. Finalists Sweden, Norway, Finland, Canada.

candidates.q: Which is the largest country that is completely surrounded by just one other country?
candidates.note: Encircled by South Africa; San Marino & Vatican are also enclaved but tiny. Finalists Lesotho, San Marino, Vatican.

candidates.q: Which United Nations member country has the fewest people of all?
candidates.note: Finalists Tuvalu, Nauru, Palau, San Marino.
